title: Steins;Gate
tagline: Time travel, tragedy, and a lab coat that will haunt you.
description: Rintaro Okabe accidentally invents a way to send messages into the past. What starts as a fun experiment spirals into a nightmare of consequence, loss, and impossible choices. The slow first half exists to make the second half absolutely devastating. Expect to lose sleep.

title: Mushishi
tagline: Slow, quiet, and will rewire what you find beautiful.
description: Ginko wanders Japan as a Mushishi — an expert on Mushi, primordial life forms invisible to most. Each episode is a self-contained folk tale. No villain, no tournament arc. Just quiet wonder and the occasional gut-punch of sadness.

title: Hunter x Hunter (2011)
tagline: Starts as shonen. Ends as a philosophical gut-shot.
description: Gon's search for his absent father starts bright and adventurous — then the Chimera Ant arc dismantles everything you thought this show was. Strategically complex, emotionally brutal, and home to one of anime's most fascinating villains.

title: Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood
tagline: The benchmark. Runs on grief and found family.
description: Two brothers pay a horrific price for forbidden alchemy and spend 64 episodes trying to reclaim what was lost. A masterclass in plotting, character work, and thematic payoff.

title: Vinland Saga
tagline: A viking revenge story that quietly becomes about peace.
description: Thorfinn grows up watching his father die and spends years sharpening his hatred. Then season two asks whether the boy forged by violence can choose something else. One of the most morally serious anime in recent memory.

title: Kaguya-sama: Love is War
tagline: Two geniuses refuse to confess. Absolute chaos ensues.
description: Two brilliant student council officers are in love and refuse to admit it — so they wage psychological warfare instead. Razor-sharp writing, perfect comedic timing, and genuinely earned emotional beats by season three.

title: Puella Magi Madoka Magica
tagline: You think it's cute. It is not cute.
description: A girl is offered a wish in exchange for becoming a magical girl. The show looks cheerful. It is not. A surgical deconstruction of the genre that turns pastel aesthetics into something genuinely haunting by episode three.

title: Nichijou
tagline: The most unhinged comedy ever animated. Perfectly mundane.
description: Three high school girls navigate ordinary life — except one friend is a robot, her inventor is an eight-year-old, and the principal wrestles deer. Animation quality that puts films to shame.

title: Attack on Titan
tagline: Humanity in a cage. The cage has teeth.
description: What begins as humans vs. giant monsters becomes one of anime's most politically complex narratives. Each season recontextualizes everything before it.

title: Demon Slayer
tagline: Stunning animation. Brutal heart. Family above everything.
description: Tanjiro's family is slaughtered by a demon and his sister is turned into one. He becomes a demon slayer to find a cure. Ufotable's animation sets a bar that most anime can only stare at from the ground.

title: Jujutsu Kaisen
tagline: Curses, chaos, and the best fight animation of the decade.
description: High schooler Yuji Itadori swallows a cursed finger and becomes host to the most powerful curse in existence. JJK is a masterclass in kinetic animation and ensemble character work.

title: Chainsaw Man
tagline: Chaotic, bloody, and somehow extremely funny.
description: Denji merges with his chainsaw devil dog to become Chainsaw Man. Violent, irreverent, and completely uninterested in being a normal shonen. One of the most stylistically distinctive anime in years.

title: Berserk (1997)
tagline: Medieval brutality and the friendship that breaks your heart.
description: Guts joins the Band of the Hawk and forms an unlikely bond with the brilliant Griffith. Berserk is the gold standard for dark fantasy — brutal, beautiful, and one of the most devastating endings in all of fiction.

title: Cowboy Bebop
tagline: Jazz, bounty hunters, and the loneliest show ever made.
description: A crew of misfit bounty hunters drifts through a colonized solar system chasing small-time crooks and their own pasts. Effortlessly cool and quietly heartbreaking.

title: Trigun
tagline: The most dangerous man alive just wants to plant flowers.
description: Vash the Stampede has a 60 billion bounty on his head and leaves a trail of destruction everywhere — despite being a pacifist who refuses to kill. Equal parts comedy and tragedy.

title: Neon Genesis Evangelion
tagline: Giant robots as a metaphor for depression. It works.
description: Teenager Shinji Ikari is forced to pilot a giant mech to fight Angels threatening humanity. The real enemy is inside his head. NGE dismantled the mecha genre and rebuilt it as psychological horror.

title: Frieren: Beyond Journey's End
tagline: An elf who outlives everyone she loves, slowly learning to grieve.
description: After a ten-year quest to defeat the Demon King, the hero's party goes home. Frieren watches her companions age and die while she barely changes. Quietly devastating.

title: One Piece
tagline: The longest found family story ever told.
description: Monkey D. Luffy sets out to become King of the Pirates and gather a crew along the way. Few anime match the emotional payoff of a fully built One Piece arc.

title: Naruto
tagline: An outcast who refused to give up until the world noticed him.
description: Naruto Uzumaki is shunned by his village for housing the demon fox. He wants to be Hokage. The emotional core — belonging, recognition, forgiveness — hits harder than almost anything else in shonen.

title: Solo Leveling
tagline: The weakest hunter becomes something the world has never seen.
description: Sung Jin-Woo is the weakest hunter in a world where portals to monster-filled dungeons appear. Then something changes. Solo Leveling is pure power fantasy done right.

title: Death Note
tagline: A notebook that kills. Two geniuses. One cat-and-mouse game.
description: Light Yagami finds a notebook that kills anyone whose name is written in it and decides to use it to create a utopia. The detective L is the only one who can stop him.

title: Code Geass
tagline: Chess pieces are people. The board is a country.
description: Exiled prince Lelouch acquires the power to make anyone obey a single command and uses it to wage war against the empire that destroyed his family.

title: Your Lie in April
tagline: A piano. A violinist. A lie. A lot of crying.
description: A piano prodigy who can no longer hear his own playing meets a free-spirited violinist who forces him back onto the stage. Breathtakingly beautiful while it destroys you.

title: AnoHana
tagline: A childhood friend who never grew up, and five people who could not move on.
description: The ghost of a childhood friend appears to a shut-in teenager who must help her fulfill her dying wish. A grief story wrapped in supernatural warmth — 11 episodes that will absolutely wreck you.

title: Violet Evergarden
tagline: A soldier who does not understand emotion, learning to write letters.
description: Violet Evergarden was a child soldier who lost her arms in war. Now she works as a letter writer for those who cannot find words. Kyoto Animation at its absolute peak.

title: Clannad: After Story
tagline: They warned you. You did not listen.
description: After Story is the second half of Clannad and the one that counts — a portrait of marriage, parenthood, loss, and the devastating weight of ordinary life.

title: Re:Zero
tagline: He dies. He resets. He suffers again. He keeps going.
description: Subaru is transported to a fantasy world and discovers he respawns after death — but no one else remembers. A psychological endurance test disguised as an isekai.

title: Overlord
tagline: The villain protagonist. The fun kind.
description: A gamer is trapped in his online RPG when the servers shut down — now fully in the body of his max-level skeletal overlord. He builds an empire. Power fantasy with a genuinely unsettling edge.

title: KonoSuba
tagline: The most useless party in any fantasy world. Somehow it works.
description: Kazuma is reincarnated in a fantasy world with a goddess who is only good at being useless, an explosion-obsessed mage, and a masochist crusader. The funniest anime of the last decade.

title: The Rising of the Shield Hero
tagline: Betrayed, enslaved, and still not giving up.
description: Naofumi is summoned as the Shield Hero and immediately framed for a crime he did not commit. He rebuilds from nothing. Shield Hero scratches the underdog itch with unusual ferocity.

title: My Hero Academia
tagline: A boy born without power in a world of superheroes.
description: Izuku Midoriya is born Quirkless in a world where 80% of people have superpowers. MHA has the cleanest shonen fundamentals of its generation — earnest, emotionally intelligent, full of great characters.

title: Toradora!
tagline: Two people who help each other fall for the wrong person.
description: Ryuji and Taiga form an unlikely alliance to help each other pursue their crushes. Toradora is the benchmark romantic comedy — you know where it's going and it still completely gets you.

title: Spy x Family
tagline: A spy, an assassin, and a telepathic child pretend to be a family.
description: Loid Forger is a spy who needs a fake family for a mission. He adopts a child who can read minds and marries an assassin. Pure wholesome fun with stylish action.

title: Bocchi the Rock!
tagline: Severe social anxiety. A guitar. Surprisingly relatable.
description: Hitori Gotoh is crushing social anxiety made flesh who discovers that playing guitar in a band might be the thing that saves her. Actually understands anxiety rather than mocking it.

title: Bleach: Thousand-Year Blood War
tagline: The arc Bleach always deserved, finally animated perfectly.
description: The final arc of Bleach — Soul Society invaded by the Quincy King — given the animation budget it always deserved.

title: ERASED
tagline: A man sent back in time to save a child he could not protect.
description: Satoru is sent back 18 years to prevent a series of childhood kidnappings. A tense, beautifully paced thriller with a mystery that actually pays off.

title: Parasyte: the maxim
tagline: An alien lives in his hand. They have to learn to coexist.
description: Shinichi Izumi's right hand is taken over by an alien parasite that fails to reach his brain. Body horror and philosophical questions about what makes something human.

title: Tokyo Ghoul
tagline: A boy who becomes what he feared most and cannot go back.
description: Ken Kaneki receives ghoul organs in a transplant after nearly dying in an attack. He is now both human and ghoul. A compelling horror drama about identity.

title: Haikyuu!!
tagline: The best sports anime ever made. Yes, including yours.
description: Hinata Shoyo is a short kid who wants to play volleyball. Haikyuu works because it makes you care deeply about both teams. The animation in the later seasons is extraordinary.

title: Mob Psycho 100
tagline: The most powerful psychic alive just wants to be normal.
description: Shigeo Mob Kageyama has overwhelming psychic powers and wants nothing more than to be ordinary. Mob Psycho 100 is about emotional intelligence over raw power.

title: One Punch Man
tagline: The strongest man alive is bored out of his mind.
description: Saitama can defeat any enemy with one punch and it's ruined his sense of purpose. A comedy about the existential hollowness of power fantasy.

title: Gintama
tagline: Feudal Japan, but with aliens. Also the funniest anime ever made.
description: Aliens have taken over Edo-period Japan and outlawed swords. Gintoki Sakata does odd jobs to pay rent. Legendary for its comedy — parodying anime tropes, breaking the fourth wall.

title: The Promised Neverland
tagline: An orphanage with a terrible secret. 11 children. One escape plan.
description: Emma and her friends live in what seems like a paradise orphanage — until they discover the truth. A survival thriller that works entirely on the intelligence of its child characters.

title: Dr. Stone
tagline: Science is the magic system. It actually works.
description: All of humanity is petrified for 3,700 years. A science genius rebuilds civilization from scratch — using actual science as their superpower. Surprisingly educational without ever being boring.

title: Noragami
tagline: A minor god, a stray shinki, and a 5-yen shrine.
description: Yato is a minor deity without a shrine who takes odd jobs for 5 yen while dreaming of becoming famous. Blends Shinto mythology with genuine emotional depth.

title: Natsume's Book of Friends
tagline: A boy who inherited his grandmother's book of spirit names.
description: Natsume Takashi can see spirits and spends the series returning their names to them. Quiet, warm, and one of the most comforting anime ever made.

title: My Teen Romantic Comedy SNAFU
tagline: A cynic, a perfectionist, and the gap between who we are and who we pretend to be.
description: Hikigaya Hachiman is a loner forced to join a service club. SNAFU is smarter than its title suggests — a painful, honest portrait of social anxiety and learning to let people in.

title: Fire Force
tagline: Firefighters, but the fires are people, and the city is a mystery.
description: In a world where humans spontaneously combust into Infernals, Shinra Kusakabe joins Special Fire Force Company 8. Gorgeous fight animation and a conspiracy plot that deepens satisfyingly.

title: World Trigger
tagline: Strategy over power. A rare thing in shonen.
description: Aliens called Neighbors invade Earth and a defense organization called Border fights back. World Trigger distinguishes itself with combat built entirely on positioning, resource management, and tactical thinking.

title: Yuri!!! on ICE
tagline: A figure skater who found himself on the ice, and someone to skate for.
description: After a crushing defeat, Japanese figure skater Yuri Katsuki is taken on by his idol Viktor Nikiforov. A romance, a sports story, and a portrait of anxiety and artistic growth.

title: Black Clover
tagline: No magic. No problem. Just an absurd amount of screaming.
description: Asta is born with no magic in a world where magic is everything. Black Clover is pure shonen energy — loud, earnest, and deeply committed to its ensemble cast.

title: Sword Art Online
tagline: Trapped in a video game where death is real.
description: Ten thousand players are trapped inside a virtual reality MMO where dying in the game means dying in real life.

title: Gurren Lagann
tagline: Drill through the heavens. Believe in the you that believes in yourself.
description: Simon and Kamina break out of their underground village and discover a world of giant mechs and alien oppressors. Gurren Lagann is pure escalating hype — it starts big and ends cosmically.

title: Made in Abyss
tagline: A hole in the earth that goes down forever. Children descend anyway.
description: Riko descends into the Abyss — a vast, mysterious chasm filled with relics and monsters — to find her missing mother. Made in Abyss looks like a children's adventure. It is one of the darkest, most brutal anime ever made.

title: That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime
tagline: Reborn as the weakest creature. Ends up running a country.
description: Satoru Mikami is reincarnated as a slime in a fantasy world. Through absorption and friendship he builds one of the most powerful nations around.

title: Mushoku Tensei
tagline: A second life taken more seriously than the first.
description: A 34-year-old shut-in dies and is reincarnated in a fantasy world with all his memories intact. Mushoku Tensei is the most technically crafted isekai ever made.

title: No Game No Life
tagline: Two genius shut-ins transported to a world decided by games.
description: Siblings Sora and Shiro are unbeatable at every game and are transported to a world where all conflict is resolved through games. Colorful, fast, and outrageously clever.

title: Log Horizon
tagline: Trapped in an MMO. Let's build a civilization.
description: Thousands of players are trapped in the MMO Elder Tale. Unlike other isekai, Log Horizon focuses on politics, economics, and society-building rather than combat.

title: 86: Eighty Six
tagline: They say there are no casualties in this war. They're lying.
description: In a nation that claims to fight an autonomous war with zero human casualties, Lena commands a squadron of soldiers the government refuses to acknowledge as human. 86 is one of the most powerful anti-war anime ever made.

title: Goblin Slayer
tagline: He does not save the world. He kills goblins.
description: A silver-ranked adventurer dedicates his entire existence to exterminating goblins after a childhood tragedy.

title: Fate/Zero
tagline: Seven mages summon legendary heroes. Only one can win the Holy Grail.
description: The Fourth Holy Grail War — mages and their summoned heroic spirits fight to the death for one wish. Fate/Zero is morally complex, beautifully animated, and asks hard questions about idealism.

title: Fate/Stay Night: UBW
tagline: Two ideologies. One battlefield. A question about what makes a hero.
description: Shirou Emiya and Rin Tohsaka enter the Fifth Holy Grail War. UBW is a philosophical debate about heroism wrapped in Ufotable's breathtaking action sequences.

title: The Devil is a Part-Timer!
tagline: The Demon King gets a job at MgRonald's.
description: The Demon King Satan is defeated and escapes to modern Tokyo, where he has no magic and must work part-time at a fast food restaurant to survive.

title: The Ancient Magus' Bride
tagline: A girl sold at auction to a mage who is not quite human.
description: Chise Hatori, a teenager with nowhere to belong, is purchased by the non-human mage Elias Ainsworth. Ancient Magus Bride is folklore-dense, visually stunning, and quietly devastating.

title: Akame ga Kill
tagline: A revolutionary army. Assassins with weapons that kill with one hit.
description: Tatsumi joins Night Raid — an assassin group fighting a corrupt empire. Akame ga Kill is brutal about killing its cast and unashamed about it.

title: Danmachi
tagline: A boy who wants to be a hero in a dungeon full of gods.
description: Bell Cranel is the weakest adventurer in a city built around a massive dungeon. DanMachi is warm, earnest adventure with strong character dynamics and satisfying power progression.

title: Future Diary
tagline: Twelve diary owners. One survival game. Zero mercy.
description: Twelve kids are locked in a game of survival: the Future Diary. Each has a diary that predicts the future in a different way. A brutal psychological thriller.

title: Psycho-Pass
tagline: In the future, your mental state determines if you're a criminal.
description: Psycho-Pass follows officers who enforce law in a future Japan where a person's mental state can be scanned to predict criminality. One of the smartest cyberpunk political thrillers in anime.

title: Ghost in the Shell: SAC
tagline: What makes a person real in a world where memory can be manufactured?
description: Ghost in the Shell: SAC follows Section 9 as they investigate cybercrime and terrorism in a world where humans can upload their consciousness. The most philosophically dense sci-fi anime ever made.

title: Higurashi: When They Cry
tagline: A rural village. The same people keep dying in different ways.
description: A group of students investigate supernatural phenomena in a rural village. Higurashi is a horror mystery where everyone keeps dying and resetting in different ways. Genuinely disturbing.

title: Kaiji: Ultimate Survivor
tagline: A loser gambles for his life. The games are psychologically brutal.
description: Kaiji Itou is a lazy slacker drowning in debt who ends up on an underground gambling ship where the losers become slaves. Kaiji is psychological gambling fiction at its most intense.

title: Monogatari Series
tagline: A boy who survived vampirism now helps girls with supernatural problems. Mostly by talking.
description: Monogatari follows Koyomi Araragi, a high school boy who survived a vampire attack and now helps girls with supernatural afflictions. Dense, dialogue-heavy, and visually inventive.

title: The Melancholy of Haruhi Suzumiya
tagline: A girl who does not know she is God runs a school club.
description: Haruhi Suzumiya is an eccentric girl who unknowingly reshapes reality around her whims. Its non-linear first broadcast order was a landmark moment in anime history.

title: Welcome to the NHK
tagline: A hikikomori convinced society is conspiring against him.
description: Sato is a 22-year-old shut-in convinced that the NHK broadcasting company is behind a conspiracy. Welcome to the NHK is brutally honest about social anxiety, depression, and the comfort of self-delusion.

title: Kino's Journey
tagline: A traveler who spends exactly three days in every country she visits.
description: Kino travels the world on a talking motorcycle named Hermes, visiting countries with bizarre and often disturbing laws and customs for exactly three days each.

title: Fruits Basket (2019)
tagline: A girl discovers her classmates are cursed to turn into animals when hugged.
description: Tohru Honda moves in with the Sohma family and discovers their secret — members transform into Chinese zodiac animals when hugged by the opposite sex.

title: Ouran High School Host Club
tagline: A scholarship student accidentally breaks an expensive vase and must join the host club.
description: Haruhi Fujioka is a practical scholarship student at an elite school who stumbles into the Host Club. Ouran is a warm, clever reverse harem parody.

title: Nana
tagline: Two women named Nana share an apartment and change each other's lives.
description: Nana Osaki and Nana Komatsu are two very different women who end up sharing an apartment in Tokyo. Nana is one of the most mature, emotionally honest portrayals of female friendship in all of anime.

title: Chihayafuru
tagline: A girl who fell in love with karuta. Also with someone she cannot have.
description: Chihaya Ayase becomes obsessed with karuta — a competitive card game based on classical poetry — and builds a club. Chihayafuru makes karuta the most intense sport you've ever watched.

title: Orange
tagline: Everyone can see their death date. A girl who wants to change hers meets a boy who gave up.
description: In a world where everyone can see their death date and cause, high schoolers Hitomi and Kakeru meet under unique circumstances.

title: Spice and Wolf
tagline: A merchant and a wolf goddess travel north arguing about economics and falling in love.
description: The Spice and Wolf follows traveling merchant Kraft Lawrence and the ancient wolf deity Holo. A rare anime about economics, trust, and slow-building romance between equals.

title: March Comes in Like a Lion
tagline: A teenage shogi prodigy. Profoundly alone. Slowly finding his way back.
description: Rei Kiriyama is a 17-year-old professional shogi player living alone, struggling with depression. March Comes in Like a Lion is one of the most honest portrayals of mental health in any medium.

title: Blue Lock
tagline: 300 strikers. One spot on Japan's national team. No mercy.
description: Japan's Football Union locks 300 top strikers in a facility and forces them to compete in deadly psychological games to forge a world-class striker. Blue Lock is the most intense sports anime in years.

title: Hajime no Ippo
tagline: A bullied kid who learns that quiet strength hits hardest.
description: Ippo Makunouchi is bullied constantly until a pro boxer teaches him to fight. One of the most detailed, emotionally grounded sports anime ever made.

title: Run with the Wind
tagline: Ten college runners. One impossible race. Zero reasons to stop.
description: A former high school track star is recruited by his eccentric roommate to form a team for the Hakone Ekiden. Quietly one of the best sports anime ever made.

title: Ping Pong the Animation
tagline: Two childhood friends. Very different reasons to play.
description: Peco and Smile are childhood friends with completely opposite approaches to table tennis. Ping Pong is the most philosophically rich sports anime ever made.

title: Slam Dunk
tagline: A delinquent joins basketball to impress a girl. Falls in love with the sport instead.
description: Hanamichi Sakuragi joins his school's basketball team after being rejected by dozens of girls. The 2022 film adaptation is genuinely stunning.

title: K-On!
tagline: Four girls start a band. The music matters less than the friendship.
description: Five high school girls start a light music club. K-On! is deceptively simple — there's no drama, just the warm accumulation of friendship and the bittersweet reality of things ending.

title: Laid-Back Camp
tagline: Girls go camping. It is as peaceful as it sounds.
description: Four girls start a camping club and go on low-key outdoor adventures in the beautiful Japanese countryside. Laid-Back Camp is therapeutic — watching it feels like sitting by a campfire with good friends.

title: Non Non Biyori
tagline: Four kids in a village with one student per grade. Peak rural comfort.
description: Renge Miyauchi and her friends live in an extremely rural village where the whole school has five students across all grades. The definitive comfort anime.

title: Barakamon
tagline: A calligrapher exiled to an island. The island fixes him.
description: Seishuu Handa is a prodigious calligrapher who punches a critic and is exiled to a rural island. The island's community slowly reminds him why he loved art in the first place.

title: Aria the Animation
tagline: A gondolier on a flooded Mars. Nothing bad happens. That is the whole show.
description: A girl on a journey to become a gondolier on a future flooded Mars rows through a slow, beautiful life. Aria is the definitive iyashikei — healing — anime. Nothing bad ever happens. That is the entire point.

title: Sweetness & Lightning
tagline: A widowed father learns to cook for his daughter.
description: A chef discovers his young daughter works at the restaurant he frequents. Sweetness and Lightning is a quiet, emotionally resonant series about grief, food, and what it means to be a present parent.

title: Food Wars!
tagline: Cooking as combat sport. Absurd and completely compelling.
description: Soma Yukihira transfers to the elite Totsuki Culinary Academy and faces off against food prodigies in intense cooking battles.

title: Natsume Yuujinchou
tagline: A boy returns names to the spirits his grandmother had bound.
description: Natsume Takashi can see spirits, just like his grandmother Reiko who made a book of contracts forcing spirits to obey her. He spends the series returning their names.

title: Sailor Moon
tagline: The original magical girl. Still holds up.
description: Usagi Tsukino is a clumsy 14-year-old who becomes Sailor Moon — a magical guardian of love and justice. The foundational magical girl series that defined a generation.

title: Cardcaptor Sakura
tagline: A girl who accidentally releases magical cards into the world and has to catch them all.
description: Sakura Kinomoto accidentally releases the Clow Cards from a magical book and must recapture them as the new Cardcaptor. A warm, gentle magical girl series with beautiful animation.

title: JoJo: Phantom Blood
tagline: The rivalry that started it all. Jonathan vs. Dio.
description: The ancient clash between vampires and vampire hunters reaches Japan when Dio Brando uses a mysterious stone mask. The first part of JoJo's Bizarre Adventure.

title: JoJo: Battle Tendency
tagline: Joseph Joestar vs. ancient gods. He is going to be obnoxious about it.
description: Joseph Joestar battles the Pillar Men — ancient, impossibly powerful beings who created the stone mask. Battle Tendency is JoJo at its most fun and bombastic.

title: JoJo: Stardust Crusaders
tagline: Jotaro Kujo vs. Dio Brando. Across the world.
description: Jotaro Kujo and his companions travel from Japan to Egypt to defeat the immortal Dio Brando, battling Stand users along the way. Stardust Crusaders introduces Stands — the series' defining power system.

title: JoJo: Diamond is Unbreakable
tagline: A serial killer just wants a quiet life. The town disagrees.
description: Josuke Higashikata defends Morioh Town from a serial killer Stand user named Kira. Diamond is Unbreakable is JoJo's most character-rich part — a murder mystery in a small town.

title: JoJo: Golden Wind
tagline: A boy with a dream of becoming the most powerful man in the Italian mafia.
description: Giorno Giovanna dreams of becoming a Gang-Star. Set in Italy, Vento Aureo is the most stylish JoJo yet, with the best soundtrack and one of the most beloved casts.

title: JoJo: Stone Ocean
tagline: Jotaro's daughter is in prison. The conspiracy is bigger than both of them.
description: Jolyne Cujoh is wrongly imprisoned and must escape while unraveling a conspiracy connected to her father Jotaro. Stone Ocean is the most emotionally personal JoJo part.

title: Spirited Away
tagline: A girl alone in a world that does not care who she is.
description: Ten-year-old Chihiro is trapped in a spirit world run by a witch and must work to save her parents. Miyazaki's masterpiece is a coming-of-age story about labour, identity, and growing up.

title: Princess Mononoke
tagline: There are no villains. Only people who want different things.
description: A young prince cursed by a dying boar god travels to the forest to find a cure and lands in the middle of a war between the forest spirits and a human settlement. Miyazaki's most morally complex film.

title: Howl's Moving Castle
tagline: A cursed seamstress, a cowardly wizard, and a castle on legs.
description: Sophie is cursed by a witch to live as an old woman and takes shelter in the moving castle of the mysterious wizard Howl. A love story about courage, self-worth, and the absurdity of war.

title: My Neighbor Totoro
tagline: Two sisters move to the countryside and meet a forest spirit.
description: Satsuki and Mei move to a new house in the countryside while their mother is in hospital. They encounter Totoro — a giant forest spirit. The most comforting film ever made.

title: Nausicaa of the Valley of the Wind
tagline: A warrior princess who talks to the monsters everyone else fears.
description: In a post-apocalyptic world covered in toxic jungle, Princess Nausicaa tries to prevent war between human kingdoms while uncovering the truth about the toxic forest.

title: Castle in the Sky
tagline: A flying castle, treasure hunters, and two kids against the world.
description: Sheeta possesses a mysterious crystal that leads her and Pazu to Laputa — a legendary floating castle. Castle in the Sky is Miyazaki's most purely adventurous film.

title: Dragon Ball Z
tagline: The reason half the world knows what a Super Saiyan is.
description: Goku and friends defend Earth from increasingly powerful threats while going Super Saiyan and screaming for extended periods. DBZ defined shonen power escalation.

title: Naruto Shippuden
tagline: The war arc hits harder than anything in shonen.
description: Naruto returns from training to find the world on the brink of a ninja world war. Shippuden is where the emotional payoff of Naruto's early setup is fully delivered. The Pain arc is devastating.

title: Rurouni Kenshin
tagline: A legendary assassin who vowed never to kill again.
description: Himura Kenshin was the deadliest swordsman of the revolution and now wanders Japan as a pacifist. The Kyoto arc is one of shonen's finest extended storylines.

title: Samurai Champloo
tagline: Edo Japan. Hip-hop beats. Three people who should not travel together.
description: A wandering girl recruits two very different swordsmen to help find a samurai who smells of sunflowers. Samurai Champloo blends Edo-era Japan with hip-hop aesthetics.

title: Dororo
tagline: A boy born without limbs, stolen by demons. He wants them back.
description: Hyakkimaru was born without 48 body parts, each traded to a demon by his father for power. Now he hunts demons to reclaim his body piece by piece.

title: Yu Yu Hakusho
tagline: A delinquent dies saving a child and becomes a spirit detective.
description: Yusuke Urameshi dies unexpectedly saving a child and is given a second chance as a Spirit Detective. A classic 90s shonen with excellent character writing.

title: Inuyasha
tagline: A girl falls into feudal Japan and meets a half-demon she should not love.
description: Kagome Higurashi falls into a well and emerges in feudal Japan, where she meets the half-demon Inuyasha. A classic long-running adventure-romance.

title: Assassination Classroom
tagline: A classroom full of assassins. Their target is their teacher.
description: A yellow octopus creature destroys the moon and threatens to destroy Earth — unless Class 3-E can assassinate him first. He is their homeroom teacher.

title: Black Butler
tagline: A boy who sold his soul for vengeance. His demon butler sees it through.
description: Young nobleman Ciel Phantomhive made a contract with a demon named Sebastian to avenge his family. Set in Victorian England, Black Butler is gothic, stylish, and darkly funny.

title: Bungo Stray Dogs
tagline: Detectives with supernatural powers, named after famous authors.
description: Atsushi Nakajima discovers the Armed Detective Agency and the dangerous Port Mafia they oppose. BSD is stylish, character-rich, and built around literary references.

title: Katanagatari
tagline: Twelve swords. Twelve episodes. One hour each. All of it incredible.
description: A wandering ninja named Shichika and a strategist named Togame travel Japan collecting 12 ultimate swords. Told in 12 hour-long episodes. Unique format, exceptional payoff.

title: Full Metal Panic!
tagline: A soldier assigned to protect a girl at school. He has no idea how school works.
description: Sousuke Sagara is a teenage military operative assigned to protect high school girl Kaname Chidori. A perfect blend of mecha thriller and school romantic comedy.

title: Oshi no Ko
tagline: The entertainment industry is not what it looks like. Neither is this show.
description: A doctor is reincarnated as the child of his favorite idol and grows up in the entertainment industry with full memories of his past life. A devastating examination of idol culture.

title: Dungeon Meshi
tagline: A party of adventurers who eat what they kill. Including dragons.
description: Laios and his party delve into a dungeon to rescue his sister, cooking and eating the monsters they defeat along the way. Dungeon Meshi is a love letter to fantasy world-building, cooking, and found family. 2024's best anime.

title: The Apothecary Diaries
tagline: A pharmacist's apprentice solves court mysteries with her nose.
description: Maomao is a pharmacist's daughter sold into the imperial court as a servant. Her knowledge of medicine and poisons quickly draws her into court intrigue and mystery.

title: Skip and Loafer
tagline: A country girl arrives in Tokyo with big dreams and zero social calibration.
description: Mitsumi Iwakura moves to Tokyo for high school with extremely specific life goals and almost no social awareness. Skip and Loafer is the warmest, most human romance of recent years.

title: Kaiju No. 8
tagline: A man who has been failing to become a hero his entire life finally gets his chance.
description: Kafka Hibino has spent years cleaning up after kaiju attacks while his childhood friend rose through the ranks. Then a small kaiju crawls into his mouth and turns him into one.

title: Hyouka
tagline: A lazy genius who avoids wasting energy solves mysteries he does not want to solve.
description: Hotaro Oreki joins the Classics Club and meets Chitanda Eru — a girl whose boundless curiosity drags him into solving school mysteries. Hyouka is a beautiful slow burn.

title: Zom 100
tagline: The zombie apocalypse is the best thing that ever happened to him.
description: Akira Tendo has been crushed by corporate overwork for years. When zombies appear, he realizes he is finally free. Zom 100 is a comedy about liberation wrapped in a zombie apocalypse.

title: Sakurasou no Pet na Kanojo
tagline: A dorm full of geniuses. One very normal person. A lot of feelings about talent.
description: Sorata Kanda moves to a special dorm full of creative geniuses and meets Shiina Mashiro — a brilliant artist with zero life skills. Sakurasou is a romance and a meditation on talent, effort, and self-worth.
